skill.name: minimalist-ui
skill.description: Design direction for clean editorial interfaces with warm monochrome color, strong typography, flat grids, and restrained motion.
skill.summary: Create refined minimalist web interfaces with editorial typography, muted color, flat components, and quiet motion.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, keylogger, weak-crypto, and reconnaissance indicators, but review found these are markdown design examples and terminology, not executable code. The only confirmed risk is a hardcoded external placeholder image URL in SKILL.md, which creates a low network dependency but no evidence of exfiltration or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: External Placeholder Image URL
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: SKILL.md recommends using a picsum.photos placeholder URL when real assets are unavailable. This is a legitimate design aid, but it can cause generated pages to load third-party images and reveal client network metadata to that service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hardcoded URL is present and would create an external network dependency if copied into generated output. The surrounding context is image placeholder guidance, not data exfiltration or covert communication.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backtick Examples Misclassified As Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static Ruby backtick detections are inline markdown code examples for CSS classes, colors, font stacks, HTML tags, and animation values. SKILL.md contains design instructions only and does not include scripts or executable command invocations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged backticks are markdown formatting around literal UI tokens, not Ruby source code. There is no shebang, script file, command interpreter call, or user-controlled command path.

low_findings.title: Keylogger Keyword Detections Are UI Terminology
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The flagged keystroke terms describe typography for code, shortcut labels, and keyboard-style UI elements. No keyboard event listeners, logging behavior, storage, or transmission of keystrokes is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines explicitly discuss visual rendering of shortcut labels and monospace text. I found no code that captures keyboard input or records user activity.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto And Reconnaissance Detections Are Contextual False Positives
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak-crypto and system-reconnaissance findings appear to match substrings inside prose, CSS values, or design terminology. The skill contains no cryptographic operations, host inspection, environment reads, or system enumeration instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is a single markdown style guide and contains no implementation code for crypto or reconnaissance. The matched lines are readable UI guidance rather than operational instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Placeholder Asset Reference
dangerous_patterns.description: A hardcoded placeholder image service appears in design guidance. This is not malicious, but generated work should replace it with approved local or project-owned assets before production use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL is clearly present and may be copied into generated interfaces. The risk is limited to third-party asset loading, not command execution or credential exposure.

content.value_statement: Generic UI prompts often produce busy layouts with heavy effects and weak typography. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code toward restrained editorial interfaces with clear spacing, flat components, and muted accents.
actual_capabilities: Defines a warm monochrome color palette with restrained pastel accents.
Specifies typography choices for body text, hero headings, code, and metadata.
Guides flat bento grids, crisp borders, compact radii, and low-shadow components.
Provides rules for buttons, tags, FAQ accordions, keyboard labels, and faux window chrome.
Directs imagery, iconography, scroll-entry motion, hover states, and animation performance.
limitations: It is a design directive, not a component library or executable template.
It does not include accessibility tests, browser tests, or responsive layout validation.
It prefers a specific editorial aesthetic and may not fit colorful brand systems.
It references an external placeholder image service that should be replaced for production.

use_cases.title: Refine a SaaS Dashboard
use_cases.description: Turn a crowded product dashboard into a calmer interface with stronger hierarchy, flatter surfaces, and clear spacing.

use_cases.title: Style a Frontend Prototype
use_cases.description: Apply consistent typography, grids, colors, and motion rules while generating React, Vue, HTML, or Tailwind screens.

use_cases.title: Create Editorial Product Pages
use_cases.description: Build polished product pages that use document-style sections, restrained imagery, and professional micro-interactions.

prompt_templates.title: Apply the Minimalist Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a single screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the minimalist-ui skill to design a clean landing page for a project management app. Keep the layout flat, typographic, and restrained.

prompt_templates.title: Revise an Existing Interface
prompt_templates.scenario: Improve a draft without changing product scope.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the minimalist-ui skill to restyle this dashboard. Preserve the content, but improve spacing, typography, borders, colors, and component states.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Component System
prompt_templates.scenario: Create reusable design rules for implementation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the minimalist-ui skill to define buttons, cards, badges, accordions, and keyboard labels for a warm monochrome design system.

prompt_templates.title: Audit for Aesthetic Drift
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a completed UI.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the minimalist-ui skill to review this frontend for violations. Identify busy effects, weak typography, generic copy, color issues, and motion problems.

output_examples.input: A request to redesign a documentation homepage.
output_examples.output: A restrained page direction with a warm off-white canvas, serif hero heading, flat feature grid, crisp dividers, and subtle scroll reveals.

output_examples.input: A request to polish a settings dashboard.
output_examples.output: A quieter dashboard plan with compact controls, clear section spacing, light borders, muted status tags, and no heavy shadows.

output_examples.input: A request to check a prototype against the style.
output_examples.output: A concise review that flags gradients, generic fonts, over-rounded buttons, bright hero backgrounds, and noisy animation choices.

best_practices: Replace placeholder image URLs with approved assets before publishing generated work.
Pair the style rules with accessibility checks for contrast, focus states, and keyboard navigation.
Use the skill when the product benefits from restrained typography and document-like structure.
anti_patterns: Do not use it for brands that require vivid gradients, playful illustration, or expressive motion.
Do not treat the style guide as a substitute for product-specific information architecture.
Do not copy external placeholder assets into production without review.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create minimalist web interfaces with editorial typography, warm neutral colors, flat components, and restrained motion.

faq.question: Does it generate working code by itself?
faq.answer: No. It guides an AI assistant while the assistant generates or revises frontend code in the requested framework.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found no malicious behavior, but noted a low-risk external placeholder image URL.

faq.question: What should teams review before production?
faq.answer: Teams should replace placeholder assets, test accessibility, and confirm the aesthetic matches their brand system.

faq.question: When should I avoid this skill?
faq.answer: Avoid it when a project needs colorful marketing visuals, playful motion, or a highly expressive brand style.
